questions.question: What should you do before working on an antenna system?
questions.reason: Isolating and securing power is a primary safety step before working on antennas and feeders.

questions.question: Why should antennas be kept clear of overhead power lines?
questions.reason: Contact with overhead electrical lines can be fatal; safe separation must always be maintained.

questions.question: What is the safest action during a thunderstorm when using an outdoor antenna?
questions.reason: Lightning risk makes operation unsafe; equipment should be disconnected and operation stopped.

questions.question: Why should the power lead to radio equipment be fitted with the correct fuse?

options.text: To protect wiring and equipment against excess current

questions.reason: A correctly rated fuse helps protect cables and equipment if a fault causes excessive current.

questions.question: What is a safe approach when working on masts, roofs, or high antenna supports?

options.text: Work alone as quickly as possible
Use suitable safety equipment and avoid working alone

questions.reason: Work at height requires proper precautions, suitable equipment, and ideally another person present.

questions.question: What should you do with loose cables in the shack or operating area?

options.text: Secure them to reduce trip and damage hazards

questions.reason: Securing cables helps prevent trips, connector damage, and accidental disconnection.

questions.question: What is a key reason to assess RF exposure around your antenna system?
questions.reason: EMF assessment helps ensure operation remains within required exposure limits.

questions.question: Why should battery terminals be protected from accidental short circuits?

options.text: To avoid dangerous high currents and heating

questions.reason: Shorting a battery can release very high current and cause burns, fire, or equipment damage.

questions.question: What is the safest approach before opening radio equipment covers?

options.text: Disconnect from mains/supply and allow capacitors to discharge

questions.reason: Isolating power and allowing stored charge to dissipate reduces electric shock risk.

questions.question: Why is adequate ventilation around power supplies and transmitters important?

options.text: It prevents overheating and reduces fire risk
It increases frequency stability by law

questions.reason: Good airflow helps prevent excessive temperatures and associated hazards.

questions.question: What should be done if RF burns or shock hazards are suspected at a station?

options.text: Stop, investigate, and fix the cause before further use

questions.reason: Hazards should be addressed before operation resumes to protect people and equipment.

questions.question: What is good safety practice when routing external feeder cables into a building?

options.text: Leave gaps and strain on connectors
Provide strain relief and suitable entry protection
Run them through hot appliance vents

questions.reason: Proper entry and strain relief reduce mechanical damage and water ingress risks.
